faq.answer: Common research dosages range from 250–500 mcg per day for general protocols, with 500–1000 mcg per day used for more intensive gut healing research. The sustained GI absorption profile may allow less frequent dosing than injectable BPC-157.

name: Fragment 176-191
description: Fragment 176-191 is a 16-amino acid peptide fragment of human growth hormone (hGH), specifically targeting the C-terminal region. It is a potent lipolytic agent that stimulates fat loss without affecting blood sugar or growth hormone levels, making it a popular research compound for metabolic studies.
mechanism: Binds to GH receptors in adipose tissue and stimulates lipolysis via JAK2/STAT5 signalling, activating hormone-sensitive lipase to break down triglycerides. Unlike full-length hGH, it lacks the diabetogenic domain, so it does not impair insulin sensitivity.

name: Hexarelin
description: Hexarelin is a potent synthetic hexapeptide growth hormone secretagogue developed as an analogue of GHRP-6. It is one of the most stable and long-acting GHRPs, resistant to enzymatic degradation, and has been studied for its potent GH-releasing effects and cardioprotective properties.
mechanism: Growth hormone secretagogue receptor (GHSR-1a) agonist. Activates the ghrelin receptor on pituitary somatotrophs, stimulating GH release via phospholipase C and intracellular calcium mobilisation. The 2-methylalanine substitution confers resistance to enzymatic degradation, extending its duration of action.

name: Ipamorelin
description: Ipamorelin is a pentapeptide growth hormone secretagogue known for its highly selective GH-releasing activity. It stimulates GH release with minimal effect on cortisol, prolactin, or appetite, making it one of the most targeted GHRPs available for research.
mechanism: Selective growth hormone secretagogue receptor (GHSR-1a) agonist. Stimulates GH release from pituitary somatotrophs with high specificity. Unlike GHRP-6, it has negligible effects on appetite (cortisol and prolactin elevation are minimal), making it a more targeted GH secretagogue for research protocols.

name: GHRP-6
description: GHRP-6 is a synthetic hexapeptide growth hormone secretagogue that potently stimulates endogenous GH release. It is one of the most well-studied GHRPs and is known for its additional orexigenic (appetite-stimulating) effects through ghrelin receptor activation.
mechanism: Ghrelin receptor (GHSR-1a) agonist. Stimulates GH release from pituitary somatotrophs and increases appetite through hypothalamic activation of NPY/AgRP neurons. It also elevates cortisol and prolactin to a moderate degree.

name: PT-141 (Bremelanotide)
description: PT-141 (Bremelanotide) is a synthetic cyclic heptapeptide analogue of Melanotan II that selectively activates melanocortin receptors MC3R and MC4R. Unlike Melanotan II, it has minimal effect on skin pigmentation (MC1R) and is primarily researched for sexual dysfunction and female hypoactive sexual desire disorder.
mechanism: Selective melanocortin receptor agonist with preference for MC3R and MC4R over MC1R. MC4R activation in the hypothalamus triggers oxytocinergic signalling pathways that modulate sexual arousal and erectile function. Reduced MC1R activity means minimal skin pigmentation side effects compared to Melanotan II.

name: Kisspeptin-10
description: Kisspeptin-10 is a 10-amino acid peptide derived from the KISS1 gene product, metastin. It is a key regulator of the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal (HPG) axis, acting as a master controller of puberty onset, reproductive function, and GnRH secretion.
mechanism: Agonist at the GPR54 (KISS1R) receptor, a G-protein-coupled receptor expressed on GnRH neurons in the hypothalamus. Activation stimulates GnRH release, which in turn drives LH and FSH secretion from the pituitary. It plays an essential role in puberty onset, reproductive function, and placental biology.

name: Cerebrolysin
description: Cerebrolysin is a peptide-based nootropic preparation derived from porcine brain tissue, containing low-molecular-weight peptides and amino acids. It is clinically approved in over 40 countries for stroke recovery, traumatic brain injury, dementia, and cognitive enhancement.
mechanism: Provides neurotrophic support by mimicking the action of endogenous neurotrophic factors (BDNF, NGF, GDNF, CNTF). It reduces excitotoxicity via NMDA receptor modulation, promotes neuroplasticity through synaptogenesis, and increases cerebral blood flow and glucose metabolism in ischaemic regions.
sequence: Complex peptide mixture from porcine brain

name: P21
description: P21 is a synthetic peptide derived from the ciliary neurotrophic factor (CNTF) that acts as a selective CDK5 (cyclin-dependent kinase 5) inhibitor. It has demonstrated potent neuroprotective and cognitive-enhancing effects in preclinical research, particularly for memory formation and synaptic plasticity.
mechanism: Selectively inhibits CDK5/p35 activity, preventing hyperphosphorylation of tau and other cytoskeletal proteins. This reduces neuroinflammation, promotes synaptic plasticity, and enhances long-term potentiation (LTP) in the hippocampus, the molecular basis of memory formation. P21 also upregulates BDNF expression.

name: Dihexa
description: Dihexa is a small synthetic peptide compound developed from angiotensin IV that acts as a potent hepatocyte growth factor (HGF) mimetic. It is one of the most potent cognitive-enhancing compounds known, capable of inducing synaptogenesis and improving memory in animal models at extremely low doses.
mechanism: Mimics the activity of hepatocyte growth factor (HGF) by binding to the c-Met receptor, activating downstream PI3K/Akt and MAPK/ERK signalling. This stimulates synaptogenesis (formation of new synapses), enhances long-term potentiation (LTP), and increases dendritic spine density in the hippocampus and cortex.

name: Noopept
description: Noopept is a synthetic dipeptide nootropic (cycloprolylglycine analogue) developed in Russia with potent cognitive-enhancing and neuroprotective properties. It is approximately 1000 times more potent than piracetam on a molar basis and has been studied for memory improvement, anxiety reduction, and neuroprotection.
mechanism: Modulates AMPA and NMDA glutamate receptor signalling, enhances BDNF and NGF expression, and increases acetylcholine release in the hippocampus and cortex. It also upregulates the expression of neurotrophin-3 (NT-3) and promotes hippocampal neurogenesis, providing both acute cognitive enhancement and long-term neuroprotection.

name: Phenylpiracetam
description: Phenylpiracetam is a phenylated analogue of piracetam developed in Russia as a cognitive enhancer and psychostimulant. It is known for its potent nootropic effects, physical performance enhancement, and its unique ability to increase resistance to cold, pain, and stress (actoprotective properties).
mechanism: Modulates nicotinic acetylcholine receptors and AMPA-type glutamate receptors, enhancing cholinergic neurotransmission in the prefrontal cortex and hippocampus. It also acts as a dopamine reuptake inhibitor and increases GABA-B receptor density, contributing to its anxiolytic and stimulant properties. Improves cerebral blood flow and glucose utilisation.

name: Pramipexole
description: Pramipexole is a D2/D3 dopamine receptor agonist used clinically in the treatment of Parkinson's disease and restless legs syndrome. In research contexts, it is studied for its neuroprotective effects, cognitive modulation, and potential applications in depression, bipolar disorder, and addiction.
mechanism: Selective full agonist at dopamine D2 and D3 receptors with highest affinity for D3. It activates G-protein-coupled inwardly rectifying potassium (GIRK) channels and inhibits adenylyl cyclase. The D3 selectivity is of particular interest in neuropsychiatric research as D3 receptors are concentrated in the limbic system.

name: Wolverine Stack BPC157 & TB 500 Blend
description: The BPC-157 and TB-500 blend is a synergistic peptide stack combining the systemic regenerative properties of TB-500 (Thymosin Beta-4) with the localised healing effects of BPC-157. This combination is widely researched for accelerated tissue repair, injury recovery, and anti-inflammatory applications across multiple tissue types.
mechanism: Synergistic action: BPC-157 promotes angiogenesis via VEGF/FGF upregulation and modulates the NO system for localised tissue repair. TB-500 sequesters actin to facilitate cell migration and differentiation while upregulating VEGF and FGF for systemic angiogenesis. Together they provide coordinated multi-tissue regenerative support.
commonDosages: 250-500 mcg BPC + 500-1000 mcg TB per day

name: Gonadorelin
description: Gonadorelin is a synthetic decapeptide identical to endogenous gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH). It is the master regulator of the reproductive hormone cascade, stimulating LH and FSH release from the pituitary gland, and is used clinically for infertility diagnostics, hypogonadism, and assisted reproduction.
mechanism: Binds to GnRH receptors on anterior pituitary gonadotrophs, activating G-protein-coupled receptor signalling (Gq/11). This stimulates the synthesis and pulsatile release of luteinizing hormone (LH) and follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH). Pulsatile administration mimics natural GnRH secretion, while continuous administration suppresses gonadotropin release via receptor desensitisation.

name: DSIP (Delta Sleep-Inducing Peptide)
description: DSIP (Delta Sleep-Inducing Peptide) is a naturally occurring nonapeptide isolated from the cerebral blood of sleeping rabbits. It is a potent regulator of sleep architecture, circadian rhythms, and stress responses, researched for its ability to promote delta (deep) sleep and modulate the HPA axis.
mechanism: Modulates GABAergic and serotonergic neurotransmission in the brainstem and hypothalamus, promoting delta wave activity in the EEG. It also suppresses the HPA axis by reducing CRH and ACTH release, lowering cortisol levels. DSIP influences the pineal gland's melatonin synthesis and resynchronises disturbed circadian rhythms.

name: Oxytocin
description: Oxytocin is a 9-amino-acid cyclic neuropeptide produced in the hypothalamus and secreted by the posterior pituitary gland. It plays a central role in social bonding, pair bonding, maternal behaviour, trust modulation, and stress regulation. As a research peptide, oxytocin is widely studied for its effects on social cognition, emotional processing, anxiety reduction, and wound healing. It acts both as a hormone in the periphery and as a neurotransmitter in the central nervous system, making it one of the most versatile peptides in behavioural neuroscience and endocrinology research. Intranasal oxytocin administration has been shown to enhance emotion recognition, increase eye gaze during social interaction, and reduce amygdala reactivity to threatening stimuli. Researchers continue to investigate its therapeutic potential for autism spectrum disorder, social anxiety, post-traumatic stress disorder, and addiction. Oxytocin exerts its effects primarily through the oxytocin receptor (OXTR), a G-protein-coupled receptor expressed throughout the limbic system, triggering intracellular calcium mobilisation and MAPK/ERK signalling cascades. In UK laboratories, oxytocin is a standard research tool for studying the neurobiology of attachment, social cognition, and emotional regulation. Its well-characterised safety profile from clinical obstetrics makes it one of the most translationally relevant neuropeptides in current research.
mechanism: Binds to oxytocin receptors (OXTR), a G-protein-coupled receptor widely expressed in the brain, uterus, and mammary tissue. Activates phospholipase C, increasing intracellular calcium and triggering downstream MAPK/ERK signalling. Modulates GABAergic and dopaminergic neurotransmission in limbic circuits including the amygdala, nucleus accumbens, and prefrontal cortex.
longDescription:
## Overview

Oxytocin is a 9-amino-acid cyclic neuropeptide (sequence CYIQNCPLG with a disulfide bridge between Cys1 and Cys6) that functions as both a hormone and a neurotransmitter. First identified in 1906 by Sir Henry Dale for its role in uterine contraction during childbirth, oxytocin has since been recognised as a key modulator of social behaviour, emotional processing, and stress regulation. It is synthesised primarily in the paraventricular and supraoptic nuclei of the hypothalamus and released into circulation via the posterior pituitary. Centrally, oxytocinergic neurons project to limbic structures including the amygdala, hippocampus, nucleus accumbens, and prefrontal cortex, where oxytocin modulates social cognition, trust, empathy, and pair bonding. UK researchers in behavioural neuroscience, psychiatry, and psychopharmacology continue to investigate oxytocin's therapeutic potential for social anxiety, autism spectrum disorder, post-traumatic stress disorder, and substance use disorders.

## Mechanism of Action

Oxytocin exerts its effects through the oxytocin receptor (OXTR), a G-protein-coupled receptor belonging to the rhodopsin-type GPCR family. OXTR activation triggers phospholipase C-mediated hydrolysis of phosphatidylinositol bisphosphate, generating inositol trisphosphate (IP3) and diacylglycerol (DAG). IP3 mobilises intracellular calcium from the endoplasmic reticulum, while DAG activates protein kinase C, leading to downstream MAPK/ERK signalling. In the central nervous system, oxytocin receptor activation modulates GABAergic inhibitory neurotransmission and dopaminergic reward signalling. In the periphery, oxytocin stimulates uterine smooth muscle contraction during labour and milk ejection from mammary glands during lactation. The vasopressin V1a receptor also binds oxytocin with lower affinity, contributing to some of its peripheral effects.

## Research & Clinical Data

Human and animal studies have demonstrated oxytocin's effects on social cognition and behaviour. Intranasal oxytocin administration has been shown to increase trust in economic games, enhance emotion recognition, improve eye gaze in social interactions, and reduce amygdala reactivity to threatening stimuli. In autism spectrum disorder research, oxytocin has shown promise for improving social responsiveness and reducing repetitive behaviours, though results have been variable across clinical trials. Preclinical studies have explored oxytocin's analgesic properties, anti-inflammatory effects in wound healing models, and cardioprotective actions during ischaemia-reperfusion injury. Intranasal dosing protocols (10–40 IU) are most common in human research.

## Dosage & Administration

Research dosages vary by route of administration. Intranasal administration at 10–40 IU per dose is standard in human behavioural research, with effects typically lasting 60–120 minutes. Systemic subcutaneous doses in animal research range from 100–500 µg per day. The plasma half-life is approximately 5 minutes, necessitating repeated or continuous administration for sustained effects. Intranasal delivery is preferred for central nervous system research due to direct nose-to-brain transport bypassing the blood-brain barrier.

## Safety & Legal

Oxytocin is for research purposes only and is not approved for human use outside of clinical settings. It has a well-characterised safety profile from decades of clinical use in obstetrics. Potential side effects at research doses include mild headache, transient hypotension, and facial flushing. Oxytocin should not be used by pregnant individuals outside of approved clinical settings. Lyophilised powder should be stored at -20°C and protected from light.

faq.question: What is oxytocin used for in research?
faq.answer: Oxytocin is widely studied for its role in social bonding, trust and empathy, anxiety reduction, stress regulation, autism spectrum disorder, and wound healing. It is one of the most extensively researched neuropeptides in behavioural neuroscience.

faq.question: How does oxytocin affect social behaviour?
faq.answer: Oxytocin modulates social cognition and behaviour by acting on oxytocin receptors in limbic brain regions including the amygdala, nucleus accumbens, and prefrontal cortex. It enhances trust, facilitates pair bonding, improves emotion recognition, and reduces amygdala reactivity to social threats.

faq.question: Is oxytocin available from UK research suppliers?
faq.answer: Yes, oxytocin is widely available from UK research peptide suppliers. It is stocked by most major UK vendors as a standard research peptide due to its extensive use in behavioural science and neuroscience research.

faq.question: What is the standard oxytocin dosage for research?
faq.answer: Intranasal dosages of 10–40 IU are standard for human behavioural research, with effects lasting 60–120 minutes. Systemic research dosages in animal models range from 100–500 µg per day. Intranasal delivery is preferred for central nervous system research.

faq.question: How is oxytocin synthesised?
faq.answer: Oxytocin is a 9-amino-acid cyclic peptide with sequence CYIQNCPLG, containing a disulfide bridge between Cys1 and Cys6. It is synthesised in the paraventricular and supraoptic nuclei of the hypothalamus as a larger precursor protein and cleaved into the active peptide during axonal transport.

name: Glow
description: Glow is a peptide blend commonly used in cosmetic research. The combination is designed to support skin health research applications including collagen synthesis and tissue regeneration studies.
mechanism: Peptide blend combining GHK-Cu with other compounds to support research into skin health, collagen production, and tissue regeneration pathways.

name: KLOW
description: KLOW is a specialized peptide complex designed for research into tissue health and regeneration. It combines multiple peptide sequences to support cellular health research applications.
mechanism: Multi-peptide complex designed to support cellular repair and tissue health pathways through combined peptide signaling mechanisms.

name: Mazdutide
description: Mazdutide is a dual GLP-1 and glucagon receptor agonist under research for metabolic conditions. It combines GLP-1-mediated glucose regulation with glucagon-mediated energy expenditure for comprehensive metabolic research.
mechanism: Dual agonist at GLP-1 and glucagon receptors. GLP-1 agonism promotes insulin secretion and satiety, while glucagon agonism increases energy expenditure and hepatic glucose production.

name: SNAP-8
description: SNAP-8 is an acetylated octapeptide designed for research into neuromuscular activity and skin health. It is a synthetic peptide that mimics the N-terminal end of SNAP-25, a protein involved in neurotransmitter release.
mechanism: SNAP-8 acts as a competitive inhibitor of SNAP-25, modulating SNARE complex formation and neurotransmitter release at the neuromuscular junction.

name: Bacteriostatic Water
description: Bacteriostatic water is sterile water containing 0.9% benzyl alcohol as a preservative. It is used in laboratory settings for reconstituting and diluting lyophilized research compounds.
mechanism: The benzyl alcohol content inhibits bacterial growth, allowing multiple uses of the same vial for reconstitution purposes in laboratory research.

name: NAD+
description: NAD+ (Nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide) is a fundamental coenzyme found in all living cells. It plays essential roles in energy metabolism, DNA repair, and cellular signaling pathways central to aging research.
mechanism: NAD+ serves as an electron carrier in redox reactions, a substrate for sirtuins (SIRT1-7), PARP enzymes, and CD38. It is critical for mitochondrial function and cellular energy homeostasis.

name: HCG
description: HCG (Human chorionic gonadotropin) is a glycoprotein hormone naturally produced during pregnancy. In research, it is studied for its LH-like activity at the LH/CG receptor involved in hormonal signaling pathways.
mechanism: HCG binds to the LH/CG receptor, mimicking luteinizing hormone activity. This stimulates steroidogenesis and supports research into reproductive endocrine pathways.

name: CJC-1295 + Ipamorelin Blend

mechanism: CJC-1295 is a GHRH analogue that stimulates endogenous GH release, while Ipamorelin is a GHRP that amplifies the GH pulse. Together they provide synergistic stimulation of the GH axis.

name: Follistatin 344
description: Follistatin 344 is a recombinant 344-amino-acid glycoprotein isoform of the naturally occurring follistatin protein, a member of the activin-binding glycoprotein family that plays a central role in regulating skeletal muscle growth. It acts as a potent myostatin (GDF-8) antagonist, binding directly to myostatin and other TGF-\u03b2 superfamily members such as activins, preventing their interaction with activin type II receptors. This inhibition removes the negative regulatory brake on muscle cell proliferation and differentiation, making follistatin 344 one of the most studied peptides in muscle growth and regeneration research. Researchers in the UK and worldwide investigate follistatin 344 for applications including muscular hypertrophy, sarcopenia, cachexia, muscle wasting disorders, Duchenne muscular dystrophy, and tissue regeneration following injury. Its ability to promote significant increases in muscle mass and strength in preclinical models has attracted considerable attention from the sports medicine and regenerative biology communities. As a recombinant protein rather than a short synthetic peptide, follistatin 344 requires careful reconstitution and cold-chain storage to maintain bioactivity.
mechanism: Binds and neutralises myostatin (GDF-8) and activins, members of the TGF-\u03b2 superfamily, preventing their interaction with activin type IIB receptors. This releases the negative regulation on satellite cell activation, myoblast proliferation, and muscle fibre growth, promoting skeletal muscle hypertrophy and regeneration.

name: Survodutide
description: Survodutide (BI 456906) is a dual glucagon/GLP-1 receptor agonist under investigation for obesity and metabolic dysfunction. It targets both glucagon and GLP-1 receptors for enhanced metabolic effects.
mechanism: Dual agonist at glucagon and GLP-1 receptors. Glucagon receptor activation increases energy expenditure, while GLP-1 activation reduces appetite and slows gastric emptying.

faq.question: What is Survodutide used for in research?
faq.answer: Survodutide is a dual glucagon/GLP-1 receptor agonist being researched for obesity, metabolic dysfunction, and NAFLD/NASH. Its dual mechanism targets both appetite suppression (GLP-1) and increased energy expenditure (glucagon), which may produce greater weight loss than GLP-1 selective agonists.

name: B7-33
description: B7-33 is a research peptide derived from relaxin, studied for its potential in fibrosis, cancer, and cardiovascular research through RXFP1 receptor modulation.
mechanism: RXFP1 receptor modulation with antifibrotic and vasodilatory properties.

faq.question: What is B7-33 used for in research?
faq.answer: B7-33 is a relaxin receptor (RXFP1) agonist studied for its potential antifibrotic, vasodilatory, and cardioprotective properties, as well as in cancer research.

name: FOXO4-DR1
description: FOXO4-DR1 is a cell-penetrating peptide that disrupts the FOXO4-p53 interaction. It is researched for its potential to selectively induce apoptosis in senescent cells, with applications in anti-aging and age-related disease research.
mechanism: Disrupts FOXO4-p53 interaction, selectively inducing apoptosis in senescent cells.

faq.question: What is FOXO4-DR1 used for in research?
faq.answer: FOXO4-DR1 is a senolytic peptide that selectively induces apoptosis in senescent cells by disrupting the FOXO4-p53 interaction. It is researched for anti-aging, age-related disease, and cellular senescence clearance.

name: ACTH 1-39
description: ACTH 1-39 (Adrenocorticotropic Hormone) is the full-length precursor peptide that stimulates the adrenal cortex to produce and release corticosteroids including cortisol, corticosterone, and aldosterone.
mechanism: Binds to the melanocortin 2 receptor (MC2R) in the adrenal cortex, stimulating the production and release of corticosteroid hormones.

faq.question: What is ACTH 1-39 used for in research?
faq.answer: ACTH 1-39 (adrenocorticotropic hormone) is used in endocrinology research to study adrenal function, corticosteroid production, stress response pathways, and the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis.

name: GLP-3 (RT)
description: GLP-3 (RT) is a research tracer analogue of GLP-1 designed for metabolic pathway studies. It shares partial structural homology with native GLP-1 and is used in receptor binding and signalling research.
mechanism: Research tracer compound that interacts with GLP-1 receptor pathways. Used for in-vitro receptor binding studies and metabolic signalling pathway research.

name: Selank + Semax Blend
description: A pre-formulated research blend combining Selank and Semax, two synthetic peptide analogues studied for cognitive function and neurological research. This blend offers a convenient single-vial format for combined administration studies.
mechanism: Selank is a synthetic heptapeptide analogue of tuftsin that modulates the enkephalin system and IL-6 expression. Semax is a synthetic analogue of ACTH(4-10) that increases brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) and modulates dopaminergic and serotonergic systems. Combined, they are studied for synergistic cognitive and neuroprotective effects.
sequence: N/A (blend of Selank TKPRPGP + Semax MEHFPGP)

name: Cagrilintide
description: Cagrilintide is a long-acting amylin analogue researched for weight management. It targets the amylin receptor and is studied in combination with GLP-1 agonists for enhanced metabolic effects.
mechanism: Long-acting amylin receptor agonist that slows gastric emptying, promotes satiety, and reduces food intake through central and peripheral pathways.

faq.question: What is Cagrilintide used for in research?
faq.answer: Cagrilintide is a long-acting amylin analogue being researched for weight management and obesity. It acts on amylin receptors to promote satiety and reduce food intake, and is often studied in combination with GLP-1 receptor agonists like semaglutide.

name: Melanotan II
description: Melanotan II is a synthetic analogue of the naturally occurring alpha-melanocyte stimulating hormone (α-MSH). It stimulates melanogenesis by binding to melanocortin receptors, leading to increased skin pigmentation. Studied for tanning, photoprotection, and sexual health applications.
mechanism: Agonist at melanocortin receptors MC1R (melanogenesis), MC3R, and MC4R (appetite/sexual function). Activation of MC1R stimulates tyrosinase and eumelanin production.
